observations.recommendation: Management agreed to revert the time deposit account to a regular deposit in current account of the general fund so that immediate utilization will not be restricted to finance priority PPAs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The travel restriction brought by the pandemic hindered the municipal treasurer to travel to Surigao city to close the time deposit account.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the MSWDO shall prioritize the
preparation and submission of the RDS to the Accounting Office
as basis for the latter to derecognize the issued inventory from the
books and to properly present the account in the financial
statements.

2.6 We recommended that Management advice the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the
implementing offices of the DOH & DILG funded projects
and return to the source agencies the balance of fund
transferred with corresponding completed programs or
projects pursuant to item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94 – 013;
b. Clarify/verify with the RHU and/or Philhealth and BFP
personnel whether the program undertakings with the
source agency covering the depository accounts maintained
by the LGU are still active and continue to be implemented,
else, the balances will be returned to the SA and close the
depository accounts. Ensure that the said program
undertakings will be supported with MOA;
c. Return to the SA the balance of the fund transfer from the
DAR-IFAD which was already phased out; and
d. Conduct regular close monitoring of the status on the
individual subsidiary ledger accounts composing the Due to
NGAs account to avoid misrepresentation of the accounts in
the FS.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a
reconciliation of the two reciprocal accounts and effect the
necessary adjusting entries to bring the balances of the Due to
Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts into agreement
in order to establish the accuracy, reliability and fair presentation
of the accounts in the financial statements.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall revert to
the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unexpended
LGU counterparts of the fully completed projects in the Trust
Fund books for proper presentation of the Cash, Trust Liability
and Equity Accounts in the financial statements and to be used for
priority programs and projects of the Municipality.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Follow-up the submission of the complete documents from
the end users covering the payments of the subject Accounts
Payable and immediately process the payments thereof to
the suppliers;
b. Remind the suppliers/creditors to claim the payments due
to them as a result of services rendered to the LGU; and
c. Revert the outstanding Accounts Payable with subject
amount of P68,344.47 to the unappropriated surplus of the
general fund if the preceding audit recommendations are
not viable.

6.10 We recommended that Management:
a. Advise the end-users and/or the personnel concern to fill out
properly and completely the POs and IARs in order to
catch–up all relevant integral information required in COA
Circular No. 96-010, Section 117 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Item A of the Manual on
the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
b. Require the suppliers or his duly authorized representative
to sign and fill out the Supplier confirmation portion and
date in the PO;
c. Advise the Inspectorate Team/Chairperson to indicate the
date of inspection in the IARs, and the Property Officer-
Designate to provide the date of receipt of the procured
items thereof; and
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the completeness
of information provided in the POs and IARs.

7.7 We recommended that the:
a. BAC through the Secretariat to post the RFQs in the
PhilGEPS website and at any conspicuous place for a
period of at least three (3) calendar days and prepare BAC
Resolution recommending award of each contract upon
determination of the Lowest Calculated and Responsive
Quotation for procurements under the shopping mode of
procurement with subject amount of more than
P50,000.00 and to attach the proof of PhilGEPS posting as
well as the BAC Resolution to the DVs; and
b. Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of
supporting documents before approving payments
thereof.

8.8 We recommended that Management ensure that all labor
components for the implementation of infrastructure projects by
administration are undertaken either in-house, by job-order or
through the pakyaw contracting system as provided under Section
5 Item 5.0 of Annex A of Government Policy Procurement Board
Resolution No. 013-2005 dated August 5, 2005.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Engineer to support the
Disbursement Vouchers and/or payroll with complete and valid
documentations.
Moreover, submit the lacking documents in the sample
transactions immediately to the Audit Team, otherwise, the
transactions will be re-evaluated on the basis of available
documents submitted which may result to audit suspensions.

9.8 We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to record the
monetization of leave credits as Terminal Leave Benefits instead of
Other Personnel Benefits account and the Municipal Budget
Officer to obligate the same in the Terminal Leave Benefits
registries.

10.5 We recommended that the Assistant Municipal Treasurer shall
prepare and maintain cash records or cashbook to record her
collections and remittances as required in Section 25, Volume II of
NGAS for LGUs and Item 7, Chapter II of RCEM.

11.7 We recommended that the MSWD Officer requires the recipients
of AICS as enumerated in the payroll to secure and submit all the
documents mentioned in Item 5 of the MOA and Item VI of MC
No. 24, s. of 2020 and for the former to accomplish the general
intake sheet of each claimant and submit copies of the same to the
office of the Auditor as supporting documents for the
disbursements for further evaluation and to ensure that the
recipients of AICS are indeed qualified beneficiaries.

12.6 We recommended that Management hasten the formulation of
clear and comprehensible guidelines covering the payments of
AICS to set as parameters in the grant thereof to prospective
clients.

13.5 We recommended that the Management shall refrain from
charging normal operating expenses against Peace and Order and
Public Safety Fund as provided in Item 3.23 of COA-DBM-DILG-
GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01. The said type of expenditures can be
paid from the MOOE and/or Capital Outlay of the General Fund
proper.

14.8 We recommended that Management direct:
a. The MDRRMO to include in the MDRRMF Investment Plan,
in a separate caption, the prior years’ MDRRMF and the
corresponding PPAs to be funded therefrom, to optimize
accessibility of the fund during disasters; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure transfer of the
unexpended balance of the MDRRMF to Special Trust Fund at
year-end or whenever possible, to reflect the correct balances
of the Cash and other affected accounts in the Financial
Statements and to make sure of cash back-up for DRRM
programs and projects.

15.8 We recommended that Management improve the utilization of the
funds to maximize government resources through proper planning
of applicable and implementable PPAs in given circumstances.

Management direct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPE and inventory thereafter. Prepare the RPCPPE & RPCI, reconcile the reports with the accounting office and effect promptly appropriate adjustments, if there are any, for a reliable and fair presentation of the accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason: Most of the PPE were already counted but the absence of the property records or historical cost of the property make it difficult in assigning the cost of the property for purposes.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall cause the submission of the BRS for CY 2020 to the Office of the Auditor and the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers on the related book reconciling items to bring the account into agreement with the records in the depository banks. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of BRS in accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 & COA Circular No. 92-125A dated March 4, 1992.

Management continue to demand the concerned Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue cash advances or refund in favor of the government the unspent amount.

observations.reason: With the ongoing pandemic, implementations of some projects were delayed/ still on-going due to the imposition of protocol issued by the government.

observations.recommendation: The Management improve the utilization rate of the 5 percent MDRRMF and ensure that utilizations are in line with the prescribed guidelines, and inform the MDRRMC of the unimplemented PPAs so that they could be of help in the implementation or to the reprogramming of those PPAs deemed not anymore implementable.

The Management through the Local School Board fully utilize or at least increase the utilization of SEF and fast track the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects, activities and incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for ECCD to support the holistic needs of the young children of the Municipality as mandated under section 7 (b) of RA No. 10410.

Management maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objectives of addressing gender issues.  (full planning and other relevant actions as appropriate)
observations.reason: Some PPA’s especially capacity development and the like were not implemented due to the imposition of protocols especially on gatherings.

observations.recommendation: Management maximize the utilization of the LCPC funds through the implementation of the LCPC related programs, projects and activities. The council will be convened to discuss on whom will be the responsible Secretariat to discuss on whom will be involved.
observations.reason: Some PPA’s were not implemented due to pandemic restrictions.

observations.recommendation: The Management direct the concerned focal persons for E – PWD to ensure maximum or at the very least increase in the utilization of the funds by revisiting the PPAs and revise them, if necessary, to come up with PPAs that are implementable to address the needs of these sector.
observations.reason: Some PPA’s not implemented due to pandemic restrictions like gathering especially on Elderly and Senior Citizens.

observations.recommendation: Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance planning and measures to enforce tax collection in order to meet or even increase to a desirable level the tax collection efficiency.

We recommended that Management give also priority to the ECCD programs, projects and activities in the budgetary formulation of SEF Budget in order not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of the intended benefits that can be derived therefrom.
observations.reason: No appropriation was allocated for CY 2020 SEF Budget due to budgetary constraint. But the ECCD PPA’s were incorporated in the LCPC Budget CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Inventory Committee to
complete the physical count of PPE and inventory as of December 31,
2020 and years thereafter. Prepare the RPCPPE & RPCI, reconcile the
reports with the accounting office and effect promptly appropriate
adjustments, if there are any, for a reliable and fair presentation of the
accounts in the Statement of Financial Position. Also, submit the report
to the Office of the Audit Team Leader for evaluation and reference in
audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to revert the time deposit
account to a regular deposit in current account of the general fund so that
immediate utilization will not be restricted to finance priority PPAs of
the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant cause the submission of the BRS for CY 2020 with
corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers on the related book
reconciling items to bring the account into agreement with the records
in the depository banks. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely
submission of BRS in accordance with Section 74 of Presidential
Decree No. 1445 & COA Circular No. 92-125A dated March 4, 1992.

We recommended that Management continue to demand the
concerned Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate and render
account for their long overdue cash advances or refund in favor of the
government the unspent amount.

We recommended the following courses of action, to wit:
a. Strictly adhere to the provision of Section 5 of DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure the optimal
utilization of 20 percent DF to help achieve the desirable socio-
economic and environmental management outcomes of the
province;
b. Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and
implementation of development projects to avoid incurring
possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation;
c. Inform the intended beneficiaries of the programmed projects so
that proper coordination will facilitate in the execution of the
projects and the benefits thereof at the earliest possible time; and
d. Prepare a catch up plan and fast tract the implementation of the
above-mentioned priority development projects.

We recommended that Management improve the utilization rate of
the 5 percent MDRRMF and ensure that utilizations are in line with
the prescribed guidelines, and inform the MDRRMC of the
unimplemented PPAs so that they could be of help in the
implementation or to the reprogramming of those PPAs deemed not
anymore priority of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management through the Local School
Board fully utilize or at least increase the utilization of SEF and fast
track the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects, and
activities pursuant to DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
series 2017 dated March 3, 2017 to ensure that intended beneficiaries
could enjoy optimum benefits therefrom.

Further, we recommended to incorporate in its budget programs and
activities intended for ECCD to support the holistic needs of the young
children of the Municipality as mandated under section 7 (b) of RA
No. 10410.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the GAD
funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects
and activities in order to attain the objectives of addressing gender issues
towards gender equality and women’s empowerment.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the
LCPC funds through the implementation of LCPC related programs,
projects and activities in order to attain the objectives for which funds
were provided.

Further, we reiterate our recommendation for the LCPC, through its
Secretariat, to establish and maintain a complete database for
children to aid in the effective identification, planning and
implementation of projects, programs and activities for the protection
of children.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned focal
persons for E – PWD to ensure maximum or at the very least increase
in the utilization of funds by revisiting the PPAs and revise them, if
necessary, to come up with PPAs that are implementable to address
the needs of these sector.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to enhance planning and measures to enforce tax collection in
order to meet or even increase to a desirable level the tax collection
efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare the above-mentioned certified list of taxpayers and submit it to the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for setting up the beginning balance of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax every year at an equal amount of one percent for each of the account out of the total amount due and collectible from taxpayers for a particular year.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conducts a physical count of PPE at the end of every year in order that appropriate adjustments of the accounts affected be promptly effected, leading to a reliable and fair presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee is still conducting physical count of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue cash Advances or refund in favor of the government the unspent amount, if applicable.

We recommended that Management recompute the cost estimates of the projects and henceforth, fast track and prioritize the implementation of the above-mentioned projects in order that the intended beneficiaries can avail of the benefits of the projects.
observations.reason: Implementation of several projects were delayed or not started due to restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management identify qualified stakeholders to be members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in order for the same to properly discharge the mandated function of the organization.
We recommended that Management establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that the much-needed data and information essential for policy making can be verified and substantiated.
observations.reason: The Monitoring and Evaluation System was already created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the full implementation of its approved GAD Plan in order not to deprive the intended recipients of the benefits that can be derived therefrom.
observations.reason: The Management were fully aware and committed to implement the PPAs as embodied in the annual GAD Budget but because of the pandemic some PPA’s were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCPC, through its Secretariat, exert sufficient efforts in the establishment and maintenance of a complete database of children to aid in the effective identification, planning and implementation of projects, programs and activities for the protection of children.
observations.reason: The establishment of the electronic database for children was not realized this year because of the travel restriction and health protocols brought about by the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned personnel attached the required supporting documents in order to substantiate the validity, regularity, and propriety of the disbursements made.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurers Office exert sufficient efforts in the collection of delinquent water service fees in order to augment the revenue of the LGU that can be used to finance various priority development projects.
We recommended that Municipal Treasury institute efficient measures and strategies to improve collection efficiency on Real Property Tax.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare the above-mentioned certified list of taxpayers and submit it to the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for setting up the beginning balance of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax every year at an equal amount of one percent for each of the account out of the total amount due and collectible from taxpayers for a particular year.
We further recommended that subsidiary ledgers/taxpayers records be updated and maintained by the Municipal Accountant

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conducts a physical count of PPE at the end of every year in order that appropriate adjustments of the accounts affected be promptly effected, leading to a reliable and fair presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate and render account for their                  long overdue cash Advances or refund in favor of the government the unliquidated amount, if applicable.

We recommended that Management recompute the cost estimates of the projects and henceforth, fast track and prioritize the implementation of the above-mentioned projects in order that the intended beneficiaries can avail of the benefits of the projects.

We recommended that Management identify qualified stakeholders to be appointed as members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in order for the same to properly discharge the mandated function of the organization.

We recommended that Management give also priority to the ECCD programs, projects and activities in the formulation of SEF Budget in order not to deprive the schoolchildren of the intended benefits that could be derived therefrom.
We recommended that Management establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that the much-needed data and information essential for policy making can be verified and substantiated.

We recommended that Management prioritize the full implementation of its approved GAD Plan in order not to                     deprive the intended recipients of the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the LCPC, through its Secretariat, exert sufficient efforts in the establishment and maintenance of a complete database for children to aid in the effective identification, planning and implementation of projects, programs and activities for the protection of children.
We recommended that the concerned personnel attached the required supporting documents in order to substantiate the validity, regularity, and propriety of the disbursements made.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurers Office exert sufficient efforts in the collection of delinquent water service fees in order to augment the revenue of the LGU that can be used to finance various priority development projects.
We recommended that Municipal Treasury institute efficient measures and strategies to improve collection efficiency on Real Property Tax.
We recommended that Management to submit within the reglementary period a  complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.reason: Physical Inventory for Office Supplies/Equipment is still on Going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to refrain from granting honoraria without legal basis and follow the provisions of law. Moreover, the Accounting Office is enjoined to meticulously check the supporting documents of the claims of honoraria before certifying the completeness and validity thereof.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the unspent LDRRMF from previous years to Trust Liability – DRRMF account as required
We recommended that the Agency to established database for GAD so appropriate programs will be executed in addressing pressing client-focused and organization-focused gender issue.
observations.reason: The CBMS data survey result is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Agriculture Office to produce the report of distribution and/or acknowledgement receipt, and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments thereafter.

We recommended to the various implementing arms of the Municipality of Libjo to properly plan, closely monitor and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishment of planned projects, programs and activities.
observations.reason:
1.) Some PPAs has not yet been implemented/ utilized because some funds are allocated for LGU Counterpart for Projects from National and Other LGUs, hence no projects has been received for CY 2019 that needed a LGU counterpart this funds is not utilize. 
2.) PPAs or funds in support for Barangay Development such as Community Development Support Program, Rehab/Improvement of Brgy. Day Care Center, Rehab/Improvement of Birthing Center, and Rehab/Improvement of Health Centers are partially implemented/utilized because it is dependent from the barangay request for such Funds.

observations.recommendation: 1.8 We recommended that Management to submit within the reglementary period a complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.

2.3	We recommended that Management refrain from granting honoraria without legal basis and follow the provisions of law. Moreover, the Accounting Office is enjoined to meticulously check the supporting documents of the claims of honoraria before certifying the completeness and validity thereof.
3.3	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the unspent LDRRMF from previous years to Trust Liability- DRRMF account as required.
4.6	We recommended that the Agency to established database for GAD so appropriate programs will be executed in addressing pressing client-focused and organization-focused gender issue.
5.5 We recommended that the Management to prioritize programs and projects that are more useful and relevant to disaster preparedness and substantially utilize their LDRRM Budget.

6.4	We recommended to the various implementing arms of the Municipality of Libjo to properly plan, closely monitor and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishment of planned projects, programs and activities.

Conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.

Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.

Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.

Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.

The inventory committee started the physical inventory of PPE.

To maintain required PPE ledger card

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2017..
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was already been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate reversion of the Accounts Payable totaling Php11,499.99 to the unappropriated surplus of the agency so that it can be appropriated to other beneficial programs, projects and activities of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was already been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Agriculture Office to produce the report of distribution and/or acknowledgement receipt, and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments thereafter.
observations.managementAction: Distribution List was already been found and adjustment was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to trace/find records and documents to facilitate review and analysis of the said account, then enforce collection or require liquidation of the same. Should all efforts fail, request for a write-off to the COA of the subject accounts and support the letter of request with (a) proof or evidences that could substantiate efforts exerted to review and validate the account; (b) list of available records and extent of validation made on the said accounts; and (c) a certification ascertaining the reasons why pertinent records and documents could not be located, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: To request for write-off
observations.reason: No records found in the accounting office to support the review and analysis of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to comply with IPSAS No.2 and reclassify short-term time deposit to Cash and Cash Equivalent in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was already been made.

observations.recommendation: Instructed the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to retrieve all stale checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting documents, and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the said documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher.  Replacement checks shall be issued following the procedures in accordance with the above-cited provision.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was already been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management defer the payment of incentives to Revenue Collectors for the time being until a legal basis and/or resolution, together with an approved guideline/criteria in the grant thereof, can be executed.
observations.managementAction: No Incentives was given for this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that claims against Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation be supported with complete necessary documents, and that such documents be duly accomplished to prove validity and regularity of such claims.
observations.managementAction: To provide more details in the client intake sheet.
observations.reason: Claims for Aics already have the necessary supporting documents such as, referral letter, Valid Id or Certificate of Indigence.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to thoroughly check the validity and completeness of the supporting documents attached in their TEV claims.

We recommended that management comply with provisions of RA 9184 in hiring a comedian and instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct corresponding withholding taxes in accordance with Section 80 of the Tax Revenue Code and Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 which implements Republic Act No. 8424, as amended.

We recommended that the members of the Local School Board allocate and utilize the Special Education Fund for programs, projects and activities that adhere to the provisions stipulated in Section 272, Article 363 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017. Also, to prioritized programs and activities that are useful for the maintenance and operation of schools.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Item 5.1.2 and Item 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the projects and activities charged in the unspent balance of LDRRMF in their annual LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: The management will include all PPA’s of unexpended LDDRMF for LDRRMFIP CY 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply, and instruct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Reports was already been prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to establish Registries for Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) with separate Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligation (RAAO) for the Quick Response Fund (QRF), Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4 (h) of New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Vol. 1 and Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Registry was already been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 37 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver the services and benefits to which the people of Libjo are entitled to.

Budgetary constraint & capability to operate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 24 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver the services and benefits to which the people of Libjo are entitled to.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management to substantially utilize their LCPC Budget for the whole year and implement the necessary programs, activities, and projects related to LCPC as incorporated in their Annual Investment Program to attain fully their LCPC objectives.
observations.managementAction: Management will substantially utilize the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly plan, closely monitor and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishment of planned projects, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management will implement programs, projects, activities as planned.

observations.recommendation: 2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2017.

3.6 We recommended the immediate reversion of the Accounts Payable totaling Php 11,499.99 to the unappropriated surplus of the agency so that it can be appropriated to other beneficial programs, projects and activities of the LGU.

4.5 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Agriculture Office to produce the report of distribution and/or acknowledgement receipt, and the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments thereafter.

5.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to trace/find records and documents to facilitate review and analysis of the said account, then enforce collection or require liquidation of the same. Should all efforts fail, request for a write-off to the COA of the subject accounts and support the letter of request with (a) proof or evidences that could substantiate efforts exerted to review and validate the account; (b) list of available records and extent of validation made on the said accounts; and (c) a certification ascertaining the reasons why pertinent records and documents could not be located, as the case may be.

6.5 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to comply with IPSAS No.2 and reclassify short-term time deposit to Cash and Cash Equivalent in the Statement of Financial Position.

7.6 We recommended that management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to retrieve all stale checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting documents, and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office of the said documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher.  Replacement checks shall be issued following the procedures in accordance with the above-cited provision.

8.6 We recommended that Management defer the payment of incentives to Revenue Collectors for the time being until a legal basis and/or resolution, together with approved guidelines/criteria in the grant thereof, can be executed.

9.9 We recommended that claims against Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation be supported with complete necessary documents, and that such documents be duly accomplished to prove validity and regularity of such claims.
10.3 Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to thoroughly check the validity and completeness   of the supporting documents attached in their TEV claims.

11.7 We recommended that Management comply with provisions of RA 9184 in hiring a comedian and instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct corresponding withholding taxes in accordance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 which implements Republic Act No. 8424, as amended.

12.7 We recommended that the members of the Local School Board allocate and utilize the Special Education Fund for programs, projects and activities that adhere to the provisions stipulated in Section 272, Article 363 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017. Also, to prioritized programs and activities that are useful for the maintenance and operation of schools.

13.4 We recommended that Management strictly comply with Item 5.1.2 and Item 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the projects and activities charged in the unspent balance of LDRRMF in their annual LDRRMFIP.

14.4 We recommended that Management strictly comply, and instruct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

15.4 We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to       establish Registries for Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) with separate Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligation (RAAO) for the Quick Response Fund (QRF), Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4 (h) of New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Vol. 1 and Section 5.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

16.3 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 37 Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver the services and benefits to which the people of Libjo are entitled to.

17.7 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 24 of Republic Act 9003 in order to deliver the services and benefits to which the people of Libjo are entitled to.

18.4 We recommended that the Management to substantially utilize their LCPC Budget for the whole year and implement the necessary programs, activities, and projects related to LCPC as incorporated in their Annual Investment Program to attain fully their LCPC objectives.

19.7 We recommended that Management properly plan, closely monitor and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishment of planned projects, programs and activities.

Conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.

Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.

Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.

Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to substantially utilize their GAD Budget for the whole year and implement the necessary programs, activities, and projects related to GAD as incorporated in their Annual GAD Plan and Budget to attain fully their GAD objectives.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that Management strictly observe the following course of actions:
1.8.1 Conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.
1.8.2 Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.
1.8.3 Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.
1.8.4 Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.

2.5	The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions. Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling  the previous cash advance.

Require the SB Members concerned to submit the required supporting documents to support their travel claims. Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to process separate vouchers for their individual reimbursement of travelling expenses duly supported with a separate Certificate of Appearance in every place of destination in accordance with their approved itinerary of travel, and accomplish separate Request for Expense Reimbursements (RERs) for any motor hire fare to ensure propriety of travel claims.

4.5	We recommended that the concerned government employees refund the excess amount of Per Diems claimed for their respective travels and that Management to avoid granting travelling allowances more than what is allowed as stipulated in Section 4 of Executive Order 298 dated March 23, 2004.
Enjoin the Management to strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184.
Direct the Procurement Officer-Designate to indicate in all purchase orders complete information required in COA Circular No. 96-010.

7.7	Require the recipient district schools to submit the above-cited documents to provide bases for monitoring the proper use of such government funds pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Adhere strictly to Section 114 and 124, Vol. 1 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS). Strengthen internal control system in recording the issuance of supplies and materials to safeguard government funds against loss or wastage that may arise resulting from improper disposition thereof due to absence and delayed preparation of required reports. Require also the committee created to conduct the physical inventory of supplies and materials at year end to account all the procured items.
observations.managementAction:
The fuel, oil, and lubricants custodian were able to submit inventory. 

The accountant recorded various purchased supplies as inventories and expensed only upon issuance to end-users.
observations.reason: The Committee on Inventory was not able to submit inventory of office supplies and was not able to conduct physical count.

observations.recommendation: Management is enjoined to compel Mr. Brigole, Ms. Cabajes, Ms. Jimenez, Mr. Llamera, Sr. and Ms. Patual to immediately require them to restitute the cash shortage incurred, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal charges, as the evidence may warrant.
observations.managementAction: Only Ms. Cabajes was able to fully remit her collections and some collectors remitted partially.
observations.reason: The other (4) collectors were not able to remit fully their cash shortages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish specific internal control system and procedures on the purchase of gasoline, oil, and lubricants to government motor vehicles in compliance to COA circular No. 77-61. Also, we recommended that Management should see to it that purchases of gasoline, oil, and lubricants should be duly supported  with complete documentation and supporting documents shall be properly filled up with necessary information pursuant to its official design before processing and approving the claims
observations.managementAction: On CY 2016 the In-charge has already recorded the fuel received, issued and balances. The issued fuel was also supported with driver’s trip ticket.
observations.reason: The purchases of gasoline, oil, and lubricants were supported with Driver’s Trip Ticket and Consumption Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the guidelines set under R.A. 9184 the “Government Procurement Reform Act”. Also, we require justifications for splitting the transaction into three payments, thereby resulting to an irregular transaction.
observations.managementAction:
The management implemented the recommendations. 

Letter of Explanation was already submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Management adhered strictly to the guidelines of R.A. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set by DSWD in the implementation of the AICS program especially with regards to the ceiling of the financial assistance. We recommend also that all financial assistance should be supported with the documents specified in the DSWD guidelines to support all the disbursement made.
observations.managementAction: Granting of pump boat hire to individual in crisis situation worth P6,000.00 per trip was already stopped. All AICS transactions were already supported with documents specified in the DSWD guidelines
observations.reason: Management adhered to DSWD guidelines in the implementation of the AICS program. All AICS transactions were supported with adequate documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management procure its common-use supplies from the PS-DBM to protect the interest of the government and achieve the objectives of Section 1.1 and 1.2 of DBM Circular No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011 and in compliance with Section 53(e) of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.  We recommend also that management should properly plan and schedule the procurement of common-use supplies so that bulk purchases, at least quarterly, could be made with the PS-DBM to avail lower prices and big discounts.
observations.managementAction: Some supplies procured were not procured in PS DBM because of low quality.
observations.reason: There were still supplies procured to various suppliers.
